title: Hyderabadi Apollo Fish Fry

ingredients.name: boneless fish fillets (rohu, seer, or tilapia), cut into 5 cm strips

subs.name: boneless chicken strips — fry 2 minutes longer; same toss-and-serve finish
rice flour — lighter, crispier coating and naturally gluten-free
omit entirely and double the cornflour for a lighter, gluten-free version

steps.text: Pat the fish strips completely dry with paper towels — any surface moisture will make the batter slide off. In a bowl, mix cornflour, maida, red chilli powder, turmeric, black pepper, ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, and salt into a thick paste. Add the fish and coat each strip well. Rest 10 minutes.

Heat oil in a deep kadai to 180°C (a pinch of batter dropped in should sizzle and rise immediately). Fry the fish strips in batches of 4–5, without crowding, for 4–5 minutes until the coating is golden and fully crisp. Drain on paper.

Pour out most of the oil, leaving about 2 tbsp in the kadai on high heat. Add curry leaves — stand back, they will spit. Add slit green chillies and onion rings and stir-fry for 2 minutes until the onions just soften and char at the edges.

Slide the fried fish back into the kadai. Sprinkle garam masala and toss vigorously for 1 minute so the fish picks up the aromatics. The coating should stay crisp. Plate immediately and top with fresh coriander.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
Keep water away from hot oil, lower food gently, avoid overfilling the vessel and never leave frying unattended.
critical_checks: Cook seafood until safely done for the species and preparation; keep it chilled before cooking.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: fish → boneless chicken strips — fry 2 minutes longer; same toss-and-serve finish; cornflour → rice flour — lighter, crispier coating and naturally gluten-free; maida → omit entirely and double the cornflour for a lighter, gluten-free version
